text:
Account snapshot: Oakbridge (mid-tier bank, fintech arm) evaluating Redwood Dedicated for customer-facing conversational assistant + search/rerank pipeline. Heavy focus on deterministic latency and reserved GPU planning. POC running on a small dedicated pool (4xA100 equiv) through Feb-March 2026.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-22: Intro + requirements call (ff-2026-01-22-87421). Captured SLA expectations: p95 < 220ms for single-turn chat (<=1024 tokens), sustained throughput 1200 QPS at peak, burst up to 2k QPS for short windows.
- 2026-01-27: SE deep-dive with Mateo — reviewed model variants, quantization tradeoffs, cold-start scenarios. SE suggested warm pool + pre-warmed KV cache for top-100 prompts.
- 2026-02-05: Security checklist handoff (drive:/Oakbridge/Redwood/SecurityFAQ.pdf). Oakbridge requires SSO (SAML), KMS integration, audit logs retained 3 years, and EU data residency for...

text: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics platform) evaluating Redwood Hosted API as fast path, then Dedicated reserved capacity for EU data residency and predictable throughput. Focus on EU-primary deployment with multi-region failover, cost/latency tradeoffs and legal data residency language. POC scoped to chat support assistant + embeddings for search.

text:
Snapshot: mid-market product analytics SaaS. Primary goal: add conversational assistant + in-app doc search that is production-grade and privacy-safe.

Sales / technical story: started with Hosted API to iterate on prompts and embedding quality; POC validated prompt templates, embedding vectors, and latency profiles. Product team now wants Dedicated in a VPC for data isolation and compliance.

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-02-10: Intro demo (Jordan, Priya) — walked through hosted flows, streaming, and embedding previews. Fireflies ff-2457.
- 2026-02-20: POC kick-off — mirror 2 product flows (support-chat, doc-search) on hosted API; Redwood provided sample batching/quant config.
- 2026-02-28: Mid-POC checkpoint call — reported p95 latency ~220ms on hosted for chat (seq len 256), embeddings quality pass for retrieval.
- 2026-03-05: Security intake opened — Echo Lattice security team...

title: Veritas Pulse Platforms
preview: Platform runs user-facing support chat (SLA: 99th pct < 250ms for interactive sessions), embedding-based search across 80M documents for compliance, and a reranking service for fraud alerts. Current hosted setup is stable but cost per token and tail-latency during morning peak are above target. Wants Dedicated upgra...
text: Platform runs user-facing support chat (SLA: 99th pct < 250ms for interactive sessions), embedding-based search across 80M documents for compliance, and a reranking service for fraud alerts. Current hosted setup is stable but cost per token and tail-latency during morning peak are above target. Wants Dedicated upgrade and Optimize to hit cost/latency goals; requires EU-region residency for EEA customers and strict audit logging for regulated lines of business.

title: WardenBridge Archive Solutions
preview: High-security gov customer. Procurement-heavy, long procurement windows. Wants on-prem air-gapped deployment or private VPC with HSM and strict retention controls. POC scoped to 3 months. AE has relationship via prior org. Quote from CISO: "cannot accept exfil risk; need full offline audit trail and FIPS modules". S...
text: High-security gov customer. Procurement-heavy, long procurement windows. Wants on-prem air-gapped deployment or private VPC with HSM and strict retention controls. POC scoped to 3 months. AE has relationship via prior org. Quote from CISO: "cannot accept exfil risk; need full offline audit trail and FIPS modules". SE feedback: heavy emphasis on reranker reproducibility and deterministic embeddings across quantized variants. Likes Redwood runtime's kernel selection idea but needs IL5 proof points. Drive links above: security FAQ + HW spec. Short-form: POC -> security review -> SOW -> procurement (GFE servers).

title: Guardian LogWorks, Inc.
preview: Summary: Guardian LogWorks is evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace an aging in-house inference pipeline for customer-facing fraud-detection and support triage. Primary decision levers are auditability and data residency. InfoSec is focused on SIEM integration (Splunk/QRadar), log schema parity with existing t...
text:
Summary: Guardian LogWorks is evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace an aging in-house inference pipeline for customer-facing fraud-detection and support triage. Primary decision levers are auditability and data residency. InfoSec is focused on SIEM integration (Splunk/QRadar), log schema parity with existing telemetry, and BYOK/KMS with HSM attestation.

Key requirements / asks (verbatim & paraphrase):
- "Customer quote" : "We need a 1:1 mapping of inference events into our SIEM with the same fields as app logs (request_id, user_id, model_version, prompt_hash, tokens_in, tokens_out, decision, latency_ms, route, node_id)".
- SIEM export: prefer syslog-over-TLS (CEF/LEEF mapping) and HTTPS push for cloud SIEMs; expect retention to be handled in SIEM but require ability to export raw logs for eDiscovery.
- Log schema: must include stable request_id (traceable to upstream app), time...

title: Northpeak AI
preview:
Account summary (Northpeak AI)
- Strong tail-latency focus; they evaluate vendors primarily on p95/p99 under mixed prompt lengths.
- Quote from deep dive: "Our short chat requests can’t get stuck behind long-context jobs. Users notice immediately." They described classic head-of-line blocking symptoms.

Workload + r...
text:
Account summary (Northpeak AI)
- Strong tail-latency focus; they evaluate vendors primarily on p95/p99 under mixed prompt lengths.
- Quote from deep dive: "Our short chat requests can’t get stuck behind long-context jobs. Users notice immediately." They described classic head-of-line blocking symptoms.

Workload + requirements
- Primary: chat generation for support agent + internal ops assistant.
- Secondary: summarization + doc search (embeddings), but their gating factor is chat tail latency.
- Traffic pattern: mix of short interactive prompts (typical chat) + periodic long-context prompts (importing large ticket threads / knowledge packs). Long-context can spike during batch workflows.
- Success metrics (as stated by them / aligned in call):
 - Improve or hold p95 TTFT for short prompts during long-context spikes.
 - Keep throughput roughly flat (they’ll tolerate small hit if p95 i...

title: Northstar Privacy AI
preview: Primary workload: high-sensitivity document search + redaction in customer support and investigations. Must process PII and regulated financial docs. Expect mostly embedding + reranking pipeline with occasional chat assistants. Latency SLOs: 150-300ms p50 generation for short queries; throughput: sustained 200 QPS f...
text: Primary workload: high-sensitivity document search + redaction in customer support and investigations. Must process PII and regulated financial docs. Expect mostly embedding + reranking pipeline with occasional chat assistants. Latency SLOs: 150-300ms p50 generation for short queries; throughput: sustained 200 QPS for embeddings during peak batch jobs. Cost sensitivity moderate; willingness to trade cost for strict residency and auditability.

text:
Topline: large defense-tier contact center integrator. Onboarding for mission-critical 24/7 ops. Must be fully air-gapped for classification boundary; cannot allow outbound tokens.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: live agent assist (real-time cues), post-call ticket summarization (structured), tool-calling to case mgmt and escalation workflows, and automated surge handling during incidents.
- Perf targets: median agent assist latency <120ms for token stream (per-agent), sustained throughput 10k tokens/sec during peak events, burst to 50k tokens/sec for incident windows.
- Reliability: automatic failover to local fallback models when primary unavailable; prefer deterministic degrade to text-only (no remote fallthrough).
- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.
- Security: FIPS-validated c...

title: Mosaic Archive Systems

- Primary problem: search performance for journalist and legal teams. Need high...
text:
Summary/CRM notes:
- Company: Mosaic Archive Systems, specializes in indexing decades of media: transcripts, closed captions, scripts, editorial metadata, and OCR from tape transfers. Content sizes are long: single assets 5k-250k tokens.
- Primary problem: search performance for journalist and legal teams. Need high recall + top-k precision. Currently using Elastic + nightly pipeline; want denser recall and reranking to cut manual curation.
- Scale: expect 20-60M chunks after fine-grained chunking (scene-level + sliding windows). Index growth steady 5-8% mo.
- Latency targets: 95th <= 300ms for search-retrieval+rerank in dedicated/private deployment for interactive UX. For legal workflows can accept 1-2s for deep rerank.
- Cost target: aiming for ~$0.01-0.05 per interactive query end-to-end (embedding lookup + reranker compute amortized). This drives conversation about quantized model...
